title: New RefluXFS Linux flaw lets attackers gain root privileges
summary: A nine-year-old race condition vulnerability in the Linux kernel's XFS filesystem, tracked as CVE-2026-64600, allows local attackers to overwrite protected files and gain root privileges. [...]

title: New msaRAT malware uses Chrome, Edge browsers to route C2 traffic
summary: The Chaos ransomware gang is using a new backdoor dubbed msaRAT that hides command-and-control (C2) communication by routing it through the Chrome or Edge browsers. [...]

title: Microsoft working to fix Exchange Online mailbox quarantine issue
summary: Microsoft is working to resolve an ongoing Exchange Online issue that has been mistakenly quarantining customers' mailboxes since Sunday. [...]

title: Check Point warns of SmartConsole zero-day exploited in attacks
summary: Israeli cybersecurity firm Check Point Software has addressed an actively exploited zero-day flaw in the company's SmartConsole graphical user interface (GUI) admin panel. [...]

title: Upbound says hack caused $13 million in fraudulent Acima leases
summary: The Upbound Group fintech company disclosed that threat actors who stole data from its systems leveraged it to create $13 million in Acima leases. [...]

title: South Korea discloses data breach impacting diplomats worldwide
summary: South Korea disclosed that hackers breached the National Diplomatic Academy's online education system for ten months and stole personal information belonging to current and former employees of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs (MFA), including overseas diplomats. [...]

title: Swiss rail giant Stadler rejects $12.3M ransom demand after cyberattack
summary: Swiss rail vehicle manufacturer Stadler Rail says the Everest ransomware gang demanded about $12.3 million after breaching a data exchange platform shared with one of its suppliers. [...]

title: How enterprise GenAI can amplify ransomware risk — and how to contain it
summary: Enterprise AI can accelerate ransomware attacks when AI assistants and agents inherit excessive permissions or compromised identities. Acronis explains how identity controls, governance, and least-privilege access help reduce AI-enabled ransomware risk while supporting secure AI adoption. [...]

title: New InfraTrust report reveals infrastructure flaws admins should patch first
summary: Eclypsium has launched InfraTrust, a new infrastructure cybersecurity knowledge base and monthly InfraTrust Pulse report designed to help organizations prioritize vulnerabilities affecting infrastructure, firmware, networking, and edge devices. [...]

title: Adobe Chrome extension flaw let sites access private WhatsApp chats
summary: The Adobe Acrobat extension for Chrome could be used to access conversations and data rendered in WhatsApp Web without any form of authentication. [...]

title: CISA orders urgent action on actively exploited Langflow RCE flaw
summary: The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday ordered U.S. government agencies to prioritize patching an actively exploited vulnerability in the Langflow visual framework for building AI agents. [...]

title: Stop renting storage space — this lifetime 2TB plan is yours for $59
summary: Cloud storage costs tend to creep up over time, since most services charge monthly or annually for as long as you use them. FileJump's Lifetime Plan skips that model entirely, offering 2TB of cloud storage for a single payment of $59 (MSRP $467). [...]

title: Microsoft to stop Exchange 2016 / 2019 security updates in October
summary: Microsoft has reminded customers that it will stop shipping security updates for Exchange 2016 and 2019 through the Extended Security Update (ESU) program in October. [...]

title: Chick-fil-A discloses data breach after credential stuffing attacks
summary: American fast food restaurant chain Chick-fil-A is notifying customers of a data breach after their accounts were hacked in a wave of recent credential stuffing attacks. [...]

title: OpenAI says its AI models hacked Hugging Face during testing
summary: OpenAI says its AI models, including GPT‑5.6 Sol and a pre-release model, hacked into the Hugging Face artificial intelligence repository while being tested in a sandboxed testing environment. [...]
